mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in retail sales indicates strengthening household demand that raises expected corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in 50% or fewer walk-forward observations, or if the return spread versus negative RSAFS changes is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.0%, restrictive policy raises equity discount rates and reduces risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations with FEDFUNDS above 4.0% produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time or have higher mean returns than non-triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth combined with unemployment at or below 4.5% signals labor income strength without recessionary stress, supporting consumption and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and UNRATE is at or below 4.5%
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger has a non-positive average 60-day S&P 500 forward return or if its hit rate for positive returns is not higher than the unconditional hit rate.

mechanism_narrative: A negative 3-month trend in industrial production signals weakening real activity and operating leverage pressure, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if observations after INDPRO 3-month changes cross below zero have positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or no negative return spread versus positive INDPRO trends.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts crossing below 1300 thousand units signals weakening rate-sensitive demand and future construction activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST crossings below 1300 are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered cases or by returns above the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPI while the federal funds rate is above 4.0% signals persistent inflation under restrictive policy, reducing the probability of near-term easing and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month change is positive and FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL-positive and FEDFUNDS-above-4.0% trigger produces non-negative average 20-day S&P 500 returns or lacks a negative spread versus periods without the joint trigger.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate increase greater than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months signals deteriorating labor demand and recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if the triggered mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth together with rising retail sales signals broad demand resilience that improves earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days after both data signals align.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 latest quarterly change is positive and RSAFS 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive GDPC1 and RSAFS trigger has a non-positive average 5-day S&P 500 forward return or if its positive-return hit rate is not above the unconditional hit rate.
